content:
- Task file marked done/passes=true and task_switch completed successfully.
- Final repo bookkeeping now: review git status, commit all changes including .ralph metadata with the required task-finished message, then push.
- No AGENTS.md addition appears necessary unless the final review turns up a reusable surprise worth codifying.

- Read the Ralph progress log, active task summary, and done-task summary.
- Confirmed there are no active bug tasks in .ralph/current_tasks.md, so bug-first does not select a bug right now.
- Resolved blockers for the authoritative managed Postgres config story: backup-removal prerequisites are done in current_tasks_done.md and task 01 of this story also passes true.
- Determined the highest-priority unblocked failing task is .ralph/tasks/story-authoritative-managed-postgres-config/02-task-make-pgtm-postgresql-conf-the-only-startup-config-entrypoint.md because it is <priority>high</priority> and now unblocked, while other remaining unblocked failing tasks are normal or low priority.
- current_task.txt does not exist yet, so create it with the selected task path next, then stop immediately.

- Created .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Wrote the selected next task path: .ralph/tasks/story-authoritative-managed-postgres-config/02-task-make-pgtm-postgresql-conf-the-only-startup-config-entrypoint.md
- Stopping immediately after selection, per Choose-Task instructions.

- Read previous progress log and .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Opened task 02-task-make-pgtm-postgresql-conf-the-only-startup-config-entrypoint.md and confirmed it is still in the initial planning phase with no TO BE VERIFIED/NOW EXECUTE marker yet.
- Started inspecting the runtime/process/managed-config files named in the task so I can write a detailed executable plan into the task file, then I should stop immediately after appending TO BE VERIFIED.

- Confirmed that task 02's description is stale: StartPostgresSpec already only carries data_dir/config_file/log_file/wait/timeout, runtime and HA start paths already materialize managed config, and the HA e2e startup test already asserts SHOW config_file points at pgtm.postgresql.conf.
- Read task 01 and saw it explicitly claims many of the code changes task 02 still describes, so the plan for task 02 needs to be an audit/verification/reconciliation plan instead of repeating the same implementation story.
- Next I am editing the task file to record that plan, with explicit steps for deep auditing remaining start paths/docs/checks, and I will end it with TO BE VERIFIED and stop immediately.

- Wrote a detailed execution plan into task 02 and ended it with TO BE VERIFIED exactly as requested.
- The plan records the key mismatch: this task file is stale and much of the described implementation already exists in the repository and is also claimed by task 01, so the later pass must perform skeptical audit/reconciliation before changing code.
- Stopping now without entering the verification or execution phases.

- Read prior progress and current task pointer for task 02.
- Next I am checking the task file phase marker so I can either verify the plan or execute without deviating from the required workflow.
- Need to stop at the correct boundary for this phase and keep the task file updated accordingly.

- Verified task 02 is in TO BE VERIFIED phase and re-checked live code paths.
- Found a concrete plan issue: repo search turns up direct-Postgres harness helpers like src/test_harness/pg16.rs that still append to postgresql.conf, but those are not pgtuskmaster-managed start paths and should not be silently pulled into task 02.
- Next I am revising the task file plan to add an explicit managed-vs-direct-postgres boundary and then I will flip the marker from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE.

- Audited the managed startup surfaces and found no remaining task-02 implementation gap in src/process/jobs.rs, src/process/worker.rs, src/runtime/node.rs, src/ha/process_dispatch.rs, src/postgres_managed.rs, src/postgres_managed_conf.rs, docs, and the HA startup e2e assertions.
- Reconciled the task file itself: updated stale research notes, clarified the managed-vs-direct-postgres harness boundary, and checked the acceptance items already satisfied by the repo.
- Next is the full required gate run: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint.
